comment: Full-viewport tile-grid regression cases for moderate zoom / high iteration configs. Each case is expanded by run-grid.mjs into every tile of the visible grid for the given viewport, replicating the Leaflet layout in client/js/MandelbrotLayer.ts (200px tiles centered on the origin point). grid-z36-i51200-report is the user-reported URL; the others are the nearest direct/high-iter and perturbation/high-iter points from the main corpus (corpus.json).

cases.note: Reported URL: mandelbrot.site/?re=...&z=36&i=51200; direct pathway, very high iterations

Direct/high-iter at the deepest direct level for 200px tiles; nudged one level shallower than the z46 source view when the cutoff moved to pixel spacing (MIN_DIRECT_PIXEL_SPACING), which put z46 on the perturbation path
Corpus direct/high-iter at moderate zoom, near the cusp
Corpus perturbation-f64/high-iter just past the threshold; exercises paired delta loop

Heaviest real pf64 view (62% interior, escaper mean 49.7k/50k trapped needle channel): ~100 s wall per grid pass on 7 workers, so it adds ~100 s per round per variant to an e2e run — use --filter to skip it during iteration; it is the ship-gate case for the heavyweight pf64 regime (added 2026-07-08, LOG items 1-4 entry)

Interior-heavy pf64 regime (68% interior cusp channel, trapped escapers mean 32k/48k): representative of the composition class the pf64 Mariani–Silver ring-fill targets — grid-z47-i50000 covers the trapped-channel regime where fills rarely land (added 2026-07-08, pf64 Mariani entry)

Heaviest real quadratic direct view in the export (177M probe iters, 77% near-max escapers, escaper mean 41k/50k border band): the direct-tier analogue of grid-z47 — throughput-bound escaper work with little interior for Mariani/periodicity to exploit; ~9 s per grid pass (added 2026-07-10, heavy-direct coverage entry)
Corpus float-exp/high-iter: covers the pathway the opt3+simd128 flag change targets
Lightest real pf64 view: guards the spawn-time cost of deep-zoom warmup work (added 2026-07-08, pf64 spawn-warmup entry)
Ultra-light deep pf64 view (200 iterations, sub-500 ms load): the worst case for any spawn-time warmup tax; accepted +23 ms here for -8.9 s on grid-z47 (added 2026-07-08, pf64 spawn-warmup entry)
